title: Java Sealed Types
description: Model a closed set of subtypes with a sealed interface and permits clause.
learningGoals: Restrict which types may implement an interface with a sealed type and permits clause.
Combine sealed types with records to describe an exhaustive set of alternatives.

completionChecks.prompt: Which Shape subtype declaration fails to compile?
choices: Compilation fails for every record that implements an interface
completionChecks.explanation: Shape permits only Circle and Square as direct subtypes, so an unlisted Triangle cannot directly implement it.

completionChecks.prompt: Why do sealed hierarchies help switch exhaustiveness?
choices: They make every subtype final automatically
They remove the need for pattern matching
The compiler knows the permitted direct subtype set
They turn every switch statement into a switch expression
completionChecks.explanation: Because the compiler knows the permitted subtype set, it can reason about whether all alternatives are handled.

completionChecks.prompt: Does a switch expression over Shape with cases for Circle and Square compile without a default?
choices: Yes, because Shape permits only Circle and Square as direct implementations
No, every switch expression over an interface must have default
No, records cannot appear in switch pattern labels
Yes, but only because sealed interfaces are converted to enums
completionChecks.explanation: The sealed permits list is the key fact; the trap is ignoring that the compiler can see the complete subtype set.
